About: It is about the sport of jumping from fixed objects, using a parachute to descend to the ground.
Affects: It affects participants by requiring them to use a parachute to descend to the ground.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with buildings, antennas, spans, and earth.
ConsistsOf: It consists of jumping from fixed objects using a parachute.
HasA: It has a parachute to descend to the ground.
It has optional freefall delay.
It has a single parachute.
It has specialized equipment and techniques.
It has a harness and container system.
It has wingsuit flying as a popular form.
It has low jumps and high jumps.
It has a slider reefing device.
It has a static line, direct bag, or P.C.A. for low jumps.
It has tandem jumps with a skilled pilot and passenger.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by jumping from fixed objects.
It is characterized by the use of a parachute.
It is characterized by being significantly more hazardous than other forms of parachuting.
It is characterized by being considered one of the most dangerous extreme sports.
It is characterized by precursors dating back hundreds of years.
It is characterized by the coining of the acronym B.A.S.E. by filmmaker Carl Boenish and others.
It is characterized by the effective birth of the sport in 1978 with filmed jumps from El Capitan.
It is characterized by the use of ram-air parachutes and the freefall tracking technique.
It is characterized by the development of specialized equipment and techniques.
It is characterized by the use of a single parachute instead of main and reserve.
HeldIn: It was held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.
IsA: It is the sport of jumping from fixed objects.
It is a popular form of wingsuit BASE jumping.
It is a form of parachuting.
It is one of the most dangerous extreme sports.
KnownAs: It is known as BASE jumping.
It is known as a sport of jumping from fixed objects.
KnownFor: It is known for being significantly more hazardous than other forms of parachuting.
It is known for being one of the most dangerous extreme sports.
OccurredAt: It occurred at St Mark's Campanile in Venice.
It occurred at El Capitan in Yosemite National Park.
It occurred at the Troll Wall.
It occurred at the Perrine Bridge in Twin Falls, Idaho.
It occurred at the Statue of Liberty.
It occurred at the Eiffel Tower.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the early eighties.
UsedFor: It is used for descending to the ground.
It is used for slowing descent.
It is used for wingsuit BASE jumping.
It is used for recreational activity.
It is used for improving control and flight characteristics in the air.
It is used for gliding over long horizontal distances.
It is used for providing additional lift.
It is used for controlling the opening speed of the parachute.
It is used for making very low jumps.
It is used for tandem BASE jumping.
UsedIn: It is used in wingsuit BASE jumping.
It is used in BASE competitions.
UsedTo: It is used to descend to the ground.
It is used to slow descent.
It is used to glide over long horizontal distances.
It is used to provide additional lift.
It is used to control the opening speed of the parachute.
It is used to enable very low jumps.
It is used to reach terminal velocity.
It is used to fly away from the cliff during freefall.
It is used to deploy parachute far away from the cliff.
It is used to jump with a passenger attached to their front.
